texto: Workers must root out the idea that by keeping the results of their labors to themselves a fortune will be assured to them. Patent fees are so much wasted money. The flying machine of the future will not be born fully fledged and capable of a flight for 1,000 miles or so. Like everything else it must be evolved gradually. The first difficulty is to get a thing that will fly at all. When this is made, a full description should be published as an aid to others. Excellence of design and workmanship will always defy competition. (1894)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lawrence Hargrave: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a negocios. O trecho “Workers must root out the idea that by keeping the results of their labors to themselves a fortun…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, aviation, evolve pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e evolve.
